summary: Addressed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES P1 blockers. March route segment canonicalization now preserves accepted path order, including descending/non-monotonic routes, and reinforcement joining now records per-commitment joined troop quantities so later partial assemblies add only their delta once. Added focused regressions for both fixes and kept the task routed to systems_architect for R2 review.

tests_run.result: ESLint passed for configured app, package, test, storybook, and tools source globs.
Node and worker-compatible deterministic hello, command/query, and save/load simulations matched.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 30 root Vitest files and 200 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-MARCH-SUPPLY-STATE-001

decisions: Preserved M4 march routeSegments array order during canonicalization because segments are ordered accepted path state, not unordered identity rows.
Added joinedCommitmentTroops ledger beside joinedCommitmentIds to track exact joined troop quantity per mobilized commitment while preserving existing display/query IDs.
Daily reinforcement joining now compares current assembled unreleased troops against previously joined troop quantity and applies only the positive delta once.

Rainy-season pause remains based on season risk captured in the accepted route plan. This keeps route-capacity assumptions stable for this task; daily reforecasting is left as a separate architecture decision if desired.
risks: R3 remains: authoritative WorldState march shape changed again with joinedCommitmentTroops; systems_architect should review before acceptance.
Save-format remains intentionally unchanged due task boundaries; save compatibility for M4 march state should remain an explicit follow-up.
requested_action: Review the R2 fixes for route segment order preservation and quantity-aware partial reinforcement joins. Do not mark ACCEPTED until architecture review passes.
